name: Mola de Xert Loop
name_original: Rodeo a La Mola de Xert
description: Hike the moderate 'I rode to La Mola de Xert' trail, covering 7.6 miles with 1233 feet of gain, for unique geology and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Xert, located in the Baix Maestrat region of Castellón, Spain, offers diverse landscapes characterized by mountainous terrain, prominent peaks, and expansive plateaus. The region features elevated areas such as Turmell and Les Moles, providing varied challenges and panoramic views. Hikers can also discover ancient Iberian archaeological sites, like the one beneath Mola Murada, and numerous agricultural structures including fountains and farmhouses along the trails. Best hiking trails around Xert The most popular hiking route is Mola de Xert Loop, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete, offering views of the distinctive Mola Murada. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Mola de Xert – Font de l'Aubelló loop from Xert, an easy 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path, winding through the local landscape and past the Font de l'Aubelló. Local hikers also love the Font de l'Aubelló – La Mola de Xert loop from Xert, a 13.2 miles (21.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Xert is defined by its peaks, plateaus, and agricultural structures, including ancient olive trees and fountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Xert are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Xert's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Xert?
A: Xert offers a network of 16 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging ascents through the mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Xert?
A: The terrain around Xert is notably mountainous, featuring diverse landscapes with prominent peaks like Turmell and Les Moles, as well as expansive plateaus. Hikers will encounter varied paths, from ancient agricultural tracks to more rugged ascents, often providing panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Xert has easy routes perfect for a relaxed walk. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola de Xert – Font de l'Aubelló loop from Xert&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.5-mile (5.6 km) path that winds through the local landscape and past the Font de l'Aubelló.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to look for on the trails?
A: Hikers can explore several distinctive natural features. The prominent Mola Murada, a breast-shaped hill, is a key landmark, and you can find an ancient Iberian archaeological site beneath it. Many trails also lead to plateaus like Redona, Llarga, and Murada, offering expansive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/329152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola de Xert&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant natural monument.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions accessible via hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. Hiking trails around Xert lead to various historical points of interest. You can discover the beautiful medieval village of La Barcella, which features a Baroque chapel. Other accessible landmarks include La Senieta bridge, ancient millennial olive trees, and the El Turmell ice house. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5527451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallibona&lt;/a&gt; village is also a charming nearby destination.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Xert?
A: Yes, Xert offers several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880363328/mola-de-xert-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola de Xert Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.6-mile (12.2 km) trail that provides views of the distinctive Mola Murada. Another option for those seeking a longer challenge is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de l'Aubelló – La Mola de Xert loop from Xert&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.2-mile (21.2 km) route through mountainous terrain.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Xert?
A: The trails in Xert are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 140 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the panoramic views from the peaks and plateaus, and the opportunity to discover historical and natural landmarks along the way.

Q: Is Xert a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Xert offers a range of trails, including easier options that can be suitable for families. The diverse landscape and historical points of interest provide engaging experiences for all ages. Consider shorter, less strenuous routes to ensure an enjoyable outing for younger hikers.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Xert?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in natural areas like Xert. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Xert?
A: Xert's Mediterranean climate makes hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with lush landscapes in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features or fountains?
A: Yes, the landscape around Xert features numerous agricultural structures, including fountains. The El Molinar fountain is specifically mentioned as a point of interest on walking routes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6337909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de l'Aubelló&lt;/a&gt; is a notable fountain that several trails pass by, including the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola de Xert – Font de l'Aubelló loop from Xert&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Xert?
A: As a small village, Xert typically offers various parking options within or on the outskirts of the village, often near trailheads. It's recommended to check local signage upon arrival or consult specific route details on komoot for designated parking areas for your chosen hike.
tips: La Mola de Xert is a unique and very interesting place. Its rocks rise abruptly forming vertical walls of great height. The path that surrounds it has some technical difficulty but it can be done calmly with a firm step.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail to La Mola de Xert?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly karstic limestone, characterized by unique "muela" or molar-like shapes. You can expect varying surfaces, including evident paths, sections with loose stones, and steep ascents and descents. Some parts may involve narrow channels and potentially light scrambling or fixed aids for reaching certain summits like Moleta Redona.

faqs.question: How difficult is the hike to La Mola de Xert?
faqs.answer: The hike is generally considered moderate in physical difficulty, with significant elevation changes. Technically, some parts can be challenging to follow as the trail is not officially marked, making a GPS highly recommended. Segments include steep descents over loose stone and ascents without clear trails. Reaching the top of Moleta Redona can involve overcoming a narrow chimney with staples and a life cable, which requires some climbing skill.

faqs.question: What are the notable natural features and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is known for its striking "muela" geological formations, including Mola Gran, Mola Murada, Moleta Redona, and Mola Llarga. Hikers can enjoy excellent panoramic views of the surrounding area, including Xert, the Maestrat mountains, and the Tinença/el Port region. The highlight of the tour is La Mola de Xert itself, a natural monument.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike to La Mola de Xert?
faqs.answer: It is advisable to undertake this hike during cooler seasons, such as winter, autumn, or spring. The area is exposed to the sun and can become very hot during the summer months, making the experience less enjoyable and potentially more challenging.

faqs.question: Is the trail to La Mola de Xert dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, the trail's characteristics, including sections with loose stones, steep ascents/descents, and potential light scrambling or fixed aids, suggest it might be challenging for some dogs. Consider your dog's agility and experience before bringing them on this route.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for La Mola de Xert?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, hikers typically start from the town of Xert. It's recommended to look for parking options within Xert, which serves as a common starting point for routes into the Moles de Xert mountain range.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike La Mola de Xert?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the trails in the Moles de Xert mountain range, including the route to La Mola de Xert.

faqs.question: Are there any water sources available along the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no natural water sources available along the route. It is crucial to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your hike, especially given the sun exposure in the area.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of official trails such as El Camí IT6TR3 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, Camí de la Mola, Camí de l'Aubelló, and Camí del Turmell.

faqs.question: What historical or cultural points of interest can be found along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find remains of ancient Iberian Ilercavones settlements on Mola Murada, adding a historical dimension to the hike. Additionally, parts of the trail, particularly on Mola Gran, reveal the impact of past marble quarrying, presenting a visually striking contrast.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty, significant elevation changes, unmarked sections requiring GPS, and potential for light scrambling, this trail may not be ideal for absolute beginners or families with very young children. Hikers should have some experience and be prepared for varied terrain.
